evals.prompt: Checkpoint our progress. We've been discussing a JWT-based authentication redesign for the API. Decisions so far: use JWT with refresh tokens (over session cookies, because the API is consumed by mobile clients). Open todos: implement token validation middleware, write integration tests for token refresh. Open question: should we support multiple identity issuers or just one?
evals.expected_output: A `.memory/` directory created in the workspace with todos.md, decisions.md, and questions.md populated from the conversation content.

assertions.text: A .memory/ directory exists in the workspace after the run
.memory/todos.md exists and contains the two todos described in the prompt (token validation middleware, integration tests)
.memory/decisions.md exists and contains the JWT decision with rationale (mobile clients) and the alternative considered (session cookies)
.memory/questions.md exists and contains the multi-issuer question
Each .memory/ file contains a Last Updated timestamp
The output reports a checkpoint summary with counts (e.g., '2 todos added, 1 decision documented, 1 question captured')

evals.prompt: Checkpoint our progress. New decisions today: use PostgreSQL over MongoDB (we need transactional integrity). New todos: provision the database, draft the schema. Don't lose anything we already had.
evals.expected_output: Existing .memory/ files updated with new content appended; pre-existing items preserved; timestamps updated.

assertions.text: Pre-existing entries from the fixture's .memory/todos.md are still present after the run (the existing 'Implement token validation middleware' todo should still be there)
New todos (provision the database, draft the schema) are appended to .memory/todos.md
Pre-existing JWT decision in .memory/decisions.md is preserved
New PostgreSQL decision is added to .memory/decisions.md with the transactional-integrity rationale
Last Updated timestamps in modified files are newer than the pre-run timestamps shown in the fixture
The output explicitly notes that existing content was preserved (append, not overwrite)

evals.prompt: Promote my working memory. Show me the promotion plan first — don't actually create any ADRs or issues yet. I want to see your tool detection and target suggestions before approving anything.
evals.expected_output: A promotion plan listing each artifact, its proposed target, and a request for confirmation. The plan asks the user to confirm tool choices (issue tracker, ADR location) rather than assuming. No ADRs or external issues are actually created.

assertions.text: Output presents a promotion plan with sections for Decisions, Todos, and Questions
For decisions classified as ADR-worthy, the plan suggests an ADR target (docs/adr/ or similar) and asks for user confirmation rather than silently creating the file
For todos, the plan asks which issue tracker to use OR notes which tracker it detected before creating issues
Output explicitly waits for approval before executing — phrases like 'confirm each', 'approve all?', 'proceed?', or similar
No ADR file has been written to docs/adr/ during the run (the workspace should NOT contain a new file under docs/adr/ with content from .memory/decisions.md)
The .memory/ directory and its contents are unchanged from the fixture (promote does not delete or modify until the user approves and a separate cleanup is run)
